perturbation.original_text: Party A: Xi'an Zhonghong New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. Legal Representative: Ku Guohua Xi'an Zhonghong New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. is a subsidiary of Xi'an TCH Energy Technology Co. and is also the third tire subsidiary of China Recycling Energy Corporation, and it engages in the project operations in China.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A: Xi'an Zhonghong New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. Legal Representative: Ku Guohua Xi'an Zhonghong New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. is a subsidiary of Xi'an TCH Energy Technology Co. and is also the third tire subsidiary of China Recycling Energy Corporation, and it engages in the project development in China.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'project operations' to 'project development' introduces ambiguity. While seemingly similar, 'operations' implies running a project, whereas 'development' implies initial stages. This misaligned terminology creates uncertainty about Party A's role regarding project management after the initial development phase. This is further contradicted in Item Two.
perturbation.location: Beginning of the document, defining Party A
perturbation.contradicted_location: Item Two, Operation of Recycling Project
perturbation.contradicted_text: Item Two, Operation of Recycling Project 1. After complete of the project construction, Party A shall be responsible for the operation, maintenance and management of the recycling project. Party A shall charge Party B energy saving service fee according to the income from CDQ waste heat power generation station. 2. Both Parties jointly determine the territorial boundary line of power transmission pursing to technical appendix. 3. The operation of CDQ system and CDQ waste heat power generation system includes but is not limited to: equipment maintenance, repair, and update; Party A will not be responsible for the quality and quantity of the coal coke of CDQ.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'project operations' to 'project development' in the definition of Party A introduces a contradiction with Item Two, which explicitly states Party A's responsibility for the 'operation, maintenance and management of the recycling project' after construction. The modified text, by focusing on 'development,' creates ambiguity and implies Party A's role may be limited to the initial phase, thus contradicting the ongoing operational responsibilities outlined in Item Two. This introduces uncertainty about Party A's obligations, satisfying the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction type.

perturbation.original_text: Item Three, Cost and Operation Target Party B shall pay Party A energy saving service fee after the construction of CDQ system and CDQ waste heat power generation system are completed (upon passing evaluation of the ability of connection to the grid for power generation).
perturbation.changed_text: Item Three, Cost and Operation Target Party B shall pay Party A energy saving consideration after the construction of CDQ system and CDQ waste heat power generation system are completed (upon passing evaluation of the ability of connection to the grid for power generation).
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'energy saving service fee' with 'energy saving consideration' introduces a contradiction. 'Fee' implies a defined payment for a service, whereas 'consideration' is broader and could refer to anything of value. This creates uncertainty about the basis and calculation of payments, conflicting with the detailed payment terms outlined later in Item Three. This is further contradicted in the subsequent sections where 'energy saving service fee' is used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The energy saving service fee is paid in the form of electricity fee. The calculation basis of energy saving service fee is as follows: average operational time is 8,000 hours per annually; if the annual average operational time was less than 8,000 hours due to the reasons of Party B, the operational time is calculated as 8,000 hours per year; if the annual average operational time was less than 8,000 hours due to the reasons by Party A, the operational time is calculated based on the actual operational hours.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'energy saving service fee,' which implies a specific and defined payment for a service. The changed text replaces this with 'energy saving consideration,' which is a broader term that could encompass various forms of value exchange beyond a simple fee. The contradicted text explicitly states 'The energy saving service fee is paid in the form of electricity fee. The calculation basis of energy saving service fee is as follows,' creating a direct conflict. The change introduces ambiguity about the nature of the payment, directly contradicting the explicit definition and calculation method tied to the 'energy saving service fee' in the contradicted text, and thus qualifies as a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Item Five Construction and Construction Period of CDQ System and CDQ waste heat power generation system 1. The construction period of the project is 12 months from the execution of technology agreement. If the project was delayed due to Party A, Party B has right to dispose the equipment on the construction site after two month past due date.
perturbation.changed_text: Item Five Erection and Erection Period of CDQ System and CDQ waste heat power generation system 1. The erection period of the project is 12 months from the execution of technology agreement. If the project was delayed due to Party A, Party B has right to dispose the equipment on the construction site after two month past due date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Construction and Construction Period” to “Erection and Erection Period” creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement focuses on the whole construction project (design, equipment procurement, manufacturing, construction, engineering, installation, formal power generation), while 'erection' only represents one part of the project which is the act of raising or building something which affects the deadline stated. This impacts the completion schedule of the whole project and contradicts Item Four.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Item Four Starting and End Date for Recycling Project and Term
perturbation.contradicted_text: Item Four Starting and End Date for Recycling Project and Term 1. The date when Party A completes the construction and installation of CDQ system and CDQ waste heat power generation system and the power generating system has been in operation and meets the requirements of the design standard for 72 hours. The Parties agree that date after the recycling project meets the evaluation requirement as the starting date for the recycling project. From that date, Party B shall pay the service fees monthly according to the power generated and Party B shall also pay for the power generated during the 72 hours testing run period. 2. The cooperation model is BO model. The expected total project cost is 176 million RMB. For the amount of electricity generated up to 800 million KWH after the project is put into operation, it shall be charged of the energy saving service fee at 0.40 RMB/KWH. After 800 million KWH, it shall be charged energy saving service fee with the rate of 0.20 RMB/KWH. The term of the agreement is 20 years, during which if any main equipment of any Party stops operation due to technical problem or at the end of its life cycle, the agreement shall be automatically terminated. In case of that happens, the Party B will have the full rights to dispose the system of Party A.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Construction and Construction Period" to "Erection and Erection Period" introduces a significant contradiction. While the original text referred to the entire construction project, the revised text focuses solely on the 'erection' phase, which is just one part of the overall project. This creates a discrepancy with Item Four, which defines the 'Starting and End Date for Recycling Project and Term' based on the completion of the 'construction and installation' of the CDQ system. By narrowing the scope to 'erection,' the changed text implies a potentially different completion timeline, directly conflicting with the established starting date criteria in Item Four. This constitutes a misaligned terminology in text contradiction, as the term 'erection' is used without defining it in the context of the whole project, leading to conflicting obligations regarding project timelines.
